denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 02/04/2026
Aetna Choice® POS II (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W101995588          Group #: GRP-1252
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-138545
Claim #: CLM-2026-201357
Date of Service (requested): 01/15/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Harborview Clinical Partners

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 01/17/2026 for Arthroscopic rotator cuff repair related to Rotator cuff tear, full-thickness (M75.101). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Arthroscopic rotator cuff repair
Diagnosis (as submitted): Rotator cuff tear, full-thickness (M75.101)
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria.
Clinical policy applied:
Guideline applied: pediatric imaging criteria (ages 0–17) referenced for this request (incorrect demographic guideline). CPB 0112 under Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) and InterQual® acute-care criteria

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
This determination is based on a plan exclusion. State coverage mandates do not alter the terms of this plan for this determination.
Our review applied the criteria in CPB 0112 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Coverage requires a defined course of conservative therapy before Arthroscopic rotator cuff repair. Documentation does not show the required duration or type of conservative care.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with conservative treatment required. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. This review used clinical criteria from our designated utilization-management program; the criteria document is available upon request.

Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file and the clinical criteria we used in this determination. Submit your request to the address on this notice or through your plan's member portal.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with an Aetna medical director before filing a written appeal.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W101995588) and reference UM-2026-138545. 

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Aetna Medical Management
Utilization Management Department
clinical_context: The 19-year-old member has Rotator cuff tear, full-thickness (M75.101). He has been treated under commercial coverage with Arthroscopic rotator cuff repair recommended as the next appropriate step. Failed 14 weeks PT; MRI supraspinatus full-thickness tear 1.8 cm. The prior-authorization packet included dated clinic notes, medication trials, and objective findings supporting medical necessity. The denial rationale does not reference those submissions. Treating clinicians document ongoing functional impairment and recommend proceeding without further delay when benefit-plan criteria have been met based on the record on file.

scenario_realism.reasoning: Diagnosis, prior treatments, and insurer administrative posture reflect everyday commercial UM denials rather than textbook perfection.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic Aetna correspondence with CPB and InterQual fingerprints and procedural tone.
